The grand coalition refers to the group of all agents
An imputation is a payoff allocation where each player i receives a non-negative share such that the sum equals the total utility v(N).
The Core is the set of stable payoff allocations where no subset of agents can collectively increase their utility more than the total awarded by the current allocation.
The Core's definition involves the characteristic function
The ε-Core is a relaxation of the Core for games with an empty Core
The Least-Core is another relaxation concept

The excess of a coalition represents the improvement in utility they can achieve by leaving the grand coalition and working together.
The LCV is the minimal ε for which the ε-core is non-empty
The LCV minimizes the incentive for any agent subset to leave the grand coalition
The LCV is found using linear programming techniques
The Shapley Value is a power index that measures each player's influence on the team's performance
The Shapley Value is unique and satisfies fairness axioms
It calculates the average marginal contribution of each player across all permutations of the team.
